title: Public vs authenticated route/permission boundary
description: Introduce an explicit decorator (e.g. @public_ok) marking routes safe to serve anonymously, with default-deny for everything else, replacing the current app-wide no-auth posture with a reviewable allow-list.
acceptance_criteria: A single decorator/mechanism marks a route as public; all other routes require an authenticated session when auth.enabled=true
The full list of public routes is enumerable from code (e.g. by grepping the decorator), not inferred from behaviour
The web/app.py module docstring ('no auth by design... localhost only') is updated to reflect the new reality once this feature ships
A test iterates all registered Flask routes and asserts every non-public one rejects an unauthenticated request when auth.enabled=true
Existing local/dev behaviour (auth.enabled=false) is unaffected — every route remains reachable as before
notes: See docs/adr/0005-public-vs-signed-in-experience-split.md, Alternatives Considered (default-deny chosen over reusing routes with conditional rendering).
